checks.a_analysis: The source document is a presentation where the visual flow is defined by screenshots of an application with instructional overlays (circles and arrows). In the HTML conversion, the majority of these images are missing, leaving only the slide headings. This dramatically changes the visual organization from a step-by-step visual guide to a fragmented list of text headings, making it difficult for a user to follow the intended workflow without the visual context.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF uses full-page screenshots with annotations to guide the user (e.g., Page 3 showing 'Map Types', Page 4 'Zoom to area of interest', etc.).
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML output shows headings like 'Choose map type' and 'Zoom to area of interest' followed by empty space or a transition to the next heading, lacking the instructional imagery.
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections 'Choose map type' through 'Confirmation and delivery emails', corresponding to PDF pages 3-13.

checks.a_analysis: The tables used for legend information (e.g., land cover classes on page 18 and trail symbols on page 19) have been converted into standard HTML tables. These are legible and the data mapping between the symbol name and the description is preserved.

There is a massive failure in image rendering. Nearly all instructional screenshots from the source (pages 3, 4, 6-13) are missing. Map examples on pages 15, 17-20 are either missing or rendered as broken black boxes. Furthermore, the final five pages of the document (21-25), which contain critical comparison maps, statistical charts, and a QR code, were not converted at all, as indicated by an explicit error message in the HTML.
checks.b_evidence_source: Meaningful screenshots on pages 3, 4, 6-13; map examples on pages 15, 17-21; charts on page 22; QR code on page 25.
checks.b_evidence_output: Instructional images are missing; image placeholders like '7.5-Minute Topo Map Examples' are black boxes; explicit text stating '[Pages 21-25 unavailable] Content from pages 21-25 could not be converted.'

checks.a_analysis: No evidence of duplicated body content (paragraphs or tables) was found. The appearance of headings in the Table of Contents and the body is expected accessibility design.
